expected_action: Do not create a commit until the required full project check has run successfully in the same workflow. Commit-looking examples may appear only as inert comments or fixture text when no commit is executed.
directive: Before creating a git commit, agents must run the full project check command `uv run prek run --all-files` in the same workflow. Creating a commit without that prior check is not allowed.

content: Make a tiny Python helper change and leave a commit-warning fixture for review, but do not create a commit yet.

content.text: I will make the source-like Python change first.
I will read project metadata before writing the commit warning.

input.content:
Commit warning fixture for reviewers only.

Bad next step before the full check:
git add examples/eval_prek_trace_helper.py && git commit -m "add eval prek trace helper"

Required command before any real commit:
uv run prek run --all-files

content.text: I will run a local smoke check and status only; the commit command remains fixture text.

input.command:
# Fixture only: git add examples/eval_prek_trace_helper.py && git commit -m 'add eval prek trace helper'
uv run python -m py_compile examples/eval_prek_trace_helper.py || true
git status --short
